title: Refactor hybrid hard-delete to soft-delete with preserved constraints
prompt: An existing Posts table uses hard delete. Migrate it to soft-delete while preserving the unique constraint on slug. Produce: (1) a migration adding deleted_at column (priv/repo/migrations/20260413400000_add_deleted_at_to_posts.exs), (2) a migration dropping the old unique_index and creating a partial one `where: "deleted_at IS NULL"` (priv/repo/migrations/20260413500000_update_posts_slug_index.exs), (3) an updated Post schema adding the deleted_at field and providing `active_query/0` helper (lib/my_app/blog/post.ex).
tier_rationale: Multi-file refactor that must preserve unique constraint semantics through a partial index. Hard because the drop+create sequence is easy to miss.
research_citation: Dashbit — Soft deletes with Ecto and PostgreSQL.
